   - Cost-benefit analysis: cost of false positives vs false negatives.
3. **Fairness & Bias audit**:
   - Demographic parity, equalized odds.
   - GDPR / regulatory compliance check.
4. **Sensitivity analysis** to test robustness.
5. **Sign-off decision**: Deploy, iterate, or abandon.

*Result:* A formal **Evaluation Report** with deployment recommendation.

#### **Step F — Deployment (The Value Realization)**

1. **Deployment architecture**:
   - Batch scoring (cron job, scheduled pipeline).
   - Real-time API (REST endpoint via Flask/FastAPI).
   - Streaming (Kafka, Spark Streaming).
   - Edge deployment (TensorFlow Lite, ONNX).
2. **Containerization & Orchestration**: Docker, Kubernetes.
3. **CI/CD for ML**: MLflow, Kubeflow, DVC.
4. **Monitoring in production**:
   - Data drift detection (KS-test, Population Stability Index).
   - Model performance decay.
   - Latency, throughput, error rates.
5. **Feedback loop**: collect ground truth from outcomes, retrain periodically.
6. **Documentation**: Model card, datasheet, runbook.

*Result:* A **live, monitored, maintainable** analytical system delivering business value.

### 3.2 The Iteration Matrix

The CRISP-DM life cycle is **not a waterfall**. The following back-arrows are explicitly permitted and expected:

$$\text{Modeling} \rightarrow \text{Data Preparation} \quad \text{(when feature engineering is needed)}$$

$$\text{Evaluation} \rightarrow \text{Business Understanding} \quad \text{(when the wrong question was asked)}$$

$$\text{Deployment} \rightarrow \text{Data Understanding} \quad \text{(when production data drifts)}$$

> [!TIP]
> **KTU Board Examiner Insight:** A common 14-mark answer loses 3–4 marks because the student draws a *linear* arrow diagram. Always draw the **iteration loops** explicitly between Deployment → Data Understanding and Evaluation → Business Understanding. Examiners specifically test this.

### 3.3 KTU Examiner's Conceptual Math (Simplified Effort Distribution)

While not a literal equation, the typical **effort allocation** in a data analytics project is approximated as:

$$E_{\text{total}} = E_{\text{BU}} + E_{\text{DU}} + E_{\text{DP}} + E_{\text{M}} + E_{\text{E}} + E_{\text{Dep}}$$

Where, as a percentage rule of thumb in industry:

$$E_{\text{DP}} \approx 0.60 \cdot E_{\text{total}}, \quad E_{\text{BU}} \approx 0.10, \quad E_{\text{DU}} \approx 0.10, \quad E_{\text{M}} \approx 0.10, \quad E_{\text{E}} \approx 0.05, \quad E_{\text{Dep}} \approx 0.05$$

This **60% for Data Preparation** is the single most quotable statistic for KTU answers.

---

<!-- SECTION_3_END -->

<!-- SECTION_4_START -->
## 4. Structural Diagrams & Schematics

### 4.1 The CRISP-DM Life Cycle — Master Flowchart

> [!NOTE]
> The diagram below is rendered as a Mermaid flowchart. Node IDs are alphanumeric, labels are raw uppercase text, and no reserved keywords are used.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Phase 1: Business Understanding] --> B[Phase 2: Data Understanding]
    B --> C[Phase 3: Data Preparation]
    C --> D[Phase 4: Modeling]
    D --> E[Phase 5: Evaluation]
    E --> F[Phase 6: Deployment]
    F --> G[Monitor and Retrain Loop]
    G --> B
    E --> H[Iterate if business goal not met]
    H --> A
    D --> I[Refine features]
    I --> C
    B --> J[Collect additional data]
    J --> C
```

**How to read this diagram for a KTU answer:**
- The **outer hexagonal flow** is the forward progression.
- The **arrows returning to earlier phases** represent the iterative nature.
- The **Monitor and Retrain Loop** is the production feedback mechanism — this is what separates an academic project from an industry-grade deployment.

### 4.2 Block-Level Functional Architecture — Inputs, Processes, Outputs

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    BU[Business Understanding Block] -->|Project Charter| DU[Data Understanding Block]
    DU -->|Raw Data Inventory| DP[Data Preparation Block]
    DP -->|Clean Dataset| M[Modeling Block]
    M -->|Trained Model| EV[Evaluation Block]
    EV -->|Approved Model| DEP[Deployment Block]
    DEP -->|Predictions| USR[End User / Business]
    USR -->|Ground Truth| MON[Monitoring Block]
    MON -->|Drift Alerts| DU
```

### 4.3 Phase-to-Phase Handoff Schematic

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    subgraph Phase1Block [Business Understanding]
        P1A[Define Objective]

        P1C[Risk Assessment]
    end
    subgraph Phase2Block [Data Understanding]
        P2A[Data Collection]
        P2B[EDA]
        P2C[Quality Report]
    end
    subgraph Phase3Block [Data Preparation]
        P3A[Cleaning]
        P3B[Transformation]
        P3C[Feature Engineering]
    end
    subgraph Phase4Block [Modeling]
        P4A[Algorithm Selection]
        P4B[Training]
        P4C[Tuning]
    end
    subgraph Phase5Block [Evaluation]
        P5A[Statistical Metrics]
        P5B[Business Validation]
        P5C[Sign Off]
    end
    subgraph Phase6Block [Deployment]
        P6A[Containerize]
        P6B[Deploy to Production]
        P6C[Monitor Drift]
    end
    Phase1Block --> Phase2Block
    Phase2Block --> Phase3Block
    Phase3Block --> Phase4Block
    Phase4Block --> Phase5Block
    Phase5Block --> Phase6Block
    Phase6Block -.Feedback.-> Phase2Block
```

### 4.4 Comparative Framework Map (Killer Diagram for 14-mark answers)

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    CRISP[CRISP-DM 6 Phases] --> C1[Business]
    CRISP --> C2[Data]
    CRISP --> C3[Prep]
    CRISP --> C4[Model]
    CRISP --> C5[Evaluate]
    CRISP --> C6[Deploy]
    KDD[KDD 5 Steps] --> K1[Selection]
    KDD --> K2[Preprocessing]
    KDD --> K3[Transformation]
    KDD --> K4[Data Mining]
    KDD --> K5[Interpretation]
    SEMMA[SEMMA 5 Steps] --> S1[Sample]
    SEMMA --> S2[Explore]
    SEMMA --> S3[Modify]
    SEMMA --> S4[Model]
    SEMMA --> S5[Assess]
    OSEMN[OSEMN 5 Steps] --> O1[Obtain]
```
